claude_code.description: Deprecated: use the project-root harnesses map keyed by harness name instead

strategy.description: How to initialize the harness config: copy syncs host settings, fresh starts clean
strategy.title: Strategy

env.description: Set container env vars when this harness is selected; overrides agent.env on key collision
env.title: Env

env_file.description: Load extra environment variables from .env-style files when this harness is selected; layered on top of agent.env_file
env_file.title: Env Files

from_env.description: Forward specific host env vars into the container when this harness is selected; layered on top of agent.from_env
from_env.title: Forward Env Vars

mount_projects.description: Bind mount the harness's host state dirs (e.g. ~/.claude/projects/ for the claude harness) into the container so auto-memory and sessions are shared across container runs and instances
mount_projects.title: Mount Host State

post_init.description: Shell commands run once after container creation when this harness is selected, appended after agent.post_init (e.g. install this harness's MCP servers)
post_init.title: Post-Init Script

pre_run.description: Shell commands run on every container start when this harness is selected, appended after agent.pre_run
pre_run.title: Pre-Run Script

claude_code.title: Claude Code

editor.description: Editor for git commits and interactive editing inside the container
editor.title: Editor

enable_shared_dir.description: Share files between host and container via ~/.clawker-share (read-only in container)
enable_shared_dir.title: Enable Shared Dir

env.description: Set container env vars directly; use from_env to forward host values instead
env.title: Env

from_env.description: Pass specific host env vars into the container (e.g. AWS_PROFILE, GITHUB_TOKEN)
from_env.title: Forward Env Vars

post_init.description: Shell commands to run after container starts but before the harness launches (e.g. install MCP servers). Useful for seeding harness config or running setup steps that require the container environment to be up. Runs only one time after container creation in the workdir with env vars loaded.
post_init.title: Post-Init Script

pre_run.description: Shell commands run on every container start, in the workdir, right before the harness CMD runs (e.g. npm install)
pre_run.title: Pre-Run Script

aliases.description: Command aliases expanded before execution; the value is appended to 'clawker' and supports $1..$N placeholders; merged across all config layers
aliases.title: Aliases

harness.description: Default harness when a command doesn't select one; any other harness stays available per run (clawker build -t HARNESS). Bare name or namespace.bundle.component address
harness.title: Default Harness

before_entrypoint.description: Add Dockerfile instructions at the very end, for this harness's image only
before_entrypoint.title: Before Entrypoint

user_commands.description: Add Dockerfile instructions as the container user, after the harness image's fragment blocks and config seeds, for this harness's image only
user_commands.title: User Commands

packages.description: Extra apt packages to install in this harness's image; not deduped against build.packages (apt install is idempotent)
packages.title: Packages

stacks.description: Extra stack definitions to render in this harness's image, after the bundle's own installer stacks
stacks.title: Stacks

harnesses.description: Per-harness build additions (stacks, packages, inject), keyed by harness name
harnesses.title: Harness Build Overlay

after_from.description: Add Dockerfile instructions while root with only the base image — e.g. apt sources, proxy config, or CA certs that package installation depends on
after_from.title: After FROM

after_packages.description: Add Dockerfile instructions while root with system packages available — e.g. compile native libraries or install tools that need those packages
after_packages.title: After Packages

after_user_setup.description: Add Dockerfile instructions while root with the container user (claude) created — e.g. set up directories, fix permissions, or configure services
after_user_setup.title: After User Setup

after_user_switch.description: Add Dockerfile instructions as the container user (claude) — e.g. install dotfiles, configure your shell, or set up user-level tools
after_user_switch.title: After User Switch

before_entrypoint.description: Add Dockerfile instructions at the very end — e.g. final environment tweaks or cleanup that must happen after everything else
before_entrypoint.title: Before Entrypoint

user_commands.description: Add Dockerfile instructions as the container user, after the harness image's fragment blocks and config seeds — e.g. add MCP servers, install plugins, or extensions
user_commands.title: User Commands

args.description: Build-time variables resolved during docker build (ARG); not available at runtime

default.description: Value used when not overridden by --build-arg at build time
default.title: Default

name.description: Build argument name (referenced as $NAME in Dockerfile instructions)
name.title: Name

args.title: Args

copy.description: Bake config files or credentials into the image (e.g. .npmrc, SSH config)

chown.description: Set file ownership (e.g. claude:claude, the unprivileged container user)
chown.title: Chown

dest.description: Where to place it inside the container
dest.title: Destination

src.description: File or directory to copy from your project
src.title: Source

copy.title: Copy

env.description: Environment variables baked into the image; use agent.env for runtime-only vars
env.title: Env

labels.description: Custom Docker labels for image metadata or tooling integration
labels.title: Labels

root_run.description: Setup commands that need root privileges (e.g. system config, additional repos)
root_run.title: Root Run

user_run.description: Setup commands that run as the container user (e.g. npm install -g, pip install)
user_run.title: User Run

packages.description: System packages (apt) needed by your project that the clawker base doesn't already install
packages.title: Packages

stacks.description: Stack definitions your root_run/user_run steps need (e.g. node, go); installed in the shared base image before your instructions run
stacks.title: Stacks

bundles.description: Installed-bundle sources (git url or local path) providing extension harnesses, stacks, and monitoring extensions; merged across all config layers

auto_update.description: Refetch this remote bundle when its source version changes, checked at the start of bundle-consuming commands (build, run, monitor up); off by default. No effect on sha-pinned or local sources.
auto_update.title: Auto Update

path.description: With url: a subdirectory of the repository to load the bundle from (monorepo case). Without url: a local directory loaded in place with no cache copy (the dev loop); a relative path resolves against the directory of the file declaring it.
path.title: Path

ref.description: Branch or tag to fetch from a remote url; sha takes precedence when both are set. Omitting both ref and sha tracks the repository's default branch. Ignored for a local path source.
ref.title: Ref

sha.description: Full 40-character commit SHA pinning a remote url for a reproducible fetch; takes precedence over ref when both are set. Ignored for a local path source.
sha.title: SHA

url.description: Git clone URL (https or ssh) of the bundle's source repository; set for a remote bundle. Mutually exclusive with a path-only local source.
url.title: URL

bundles.title: Bundles

strategy.description: How to initialize the harness config: copy syncs host settings, fresh starts clean
strategy.title: Strategy

env.description: Set container env vars when this harness is selected; overrides agent.env on key collision
env.title: Env

env_file.description: Load extra environment variables from .env-style files when this harness is selected; layered on top of agent.env_file
env_file.title: Env Files

from_env.description: Forward specific host env vars into the container when this harness is selected; layered on top of agent.from_env
from_env.title: Forward Env Vars

mount_projects.description: Bind mount the harness's host state dirs (e.g. ~/.claude/projects/ for the claude harness) into the container so auto-memory and sessions are shared across container runs and instances
mount_projects.title: Mount Host State

post_init.description: Shell commands run once after container creation when this harness is selected, appended after agent.post_init (e.g. install this harness's MCP servers)
post_init.title: Post-Init Script

pre_run.description: Shell commands run on every container start when this harness is selected, appended after agent.pre_run
pre_run.title: Pre-Run Script

harnesses.description: Per-harness container initialization settings, keyed by harness name
harnesses.title: Harnesses

extensions.description: Monitoring extensions this project contributes to the monitoring stack, by name or qualified namespace.bundle.component address; the highest config layer that sets this wins
extensions.title: Monitoring Extensions

name.description: Override the project slug derived from the directory name (set this when the directory name isn't a good clawker identifier — e.g. dots, spaces, unicode)
name.title: Project Name

cap_add.description: Extra Linux capabilities for the agent container. Empty by default — the eBPF firewall is attached from outside, so no in-container caps are needed. Add e.g. SYS_PTRACE only if your workflow requires it.
cap_add.title: Cap Add

docker_socket.description: Mount the host Docker socket (DooD, not DinD) — lets the container manage sibling containers but is a security risk
docker_socket.title: Docker Socket

enable_host_proxy.description: Run a proxy for browser-based auth flows and credential forwarding from the host
enable_host_proxy.title: Host Proxy

add_domains.description: Shorthand: domains the container can reach over HTTPS (converted to https+port-443 rules)
add_domains.title: Firewall Domains

rules.description: Full egress rules with protocol, port, and path control

action.description: Allow or deny traffic to this destination (default: allow)
action.title: Action

dst.description: Domain or IP the container needs to reach (e.g. api.github.com, registry.npmjs.org)
dst.title: Destination

insecure_skip_tls_verify.description: Accept a self-signed/untrusted upstream TLS cert for this destination (default: false). Use only for trusted local-dev endpoints.
insecure_skip_tls_verify.title: Insecure Skip TLS Verify

path_default.description: What to do with HTTP paths that don't match any path rule (allow or deny)
path_default.title: Path Default

path_rules.description: Fine-grained path filtering (only applies to http/https/ws/wss)

action.description: Whether to allow or deny requests matching this path
action.title: Action

methods.description: HTTP methods this path rule applies to (e.g. GET, HEAD); empty = all methods. Only meaningful for http/https/ws/wss.
methods.title: Methods

path.description: URL path to match: a literal prefix starting with / (e.g. /v1/api), or — when prefixed with ~ — an RE2 regex matched full-string for exact/anchored matching (e.g. ~/repos/(a|b)/?)
path.title: Path

path_rules.title: Path Rules

port.description: Destination port: a single port (443) or an inclusive range (9000-9100); empty = protocol default
port.title: Port

proto.description: L7 protocol: https (TLS-MITM, default), http (plaintext HCM), ws/wss (websocket over http/https), ssh, tcp, udp, or any opaque L7 name for TCP pass-through
proto.title: Protocol

rules.title: Rules

copy_git_config.description: Sync your host .gitconfig (aliases, user.name, user.email) into the container
copy_git_config.title: Copy Git Config

forward_gpg.description: Let git sign commits using your host GPG keys
forward_gpg.title: Forward GPG

forward_https.description: Let git clone/push use your host HTTPS credentials (via host proxy)
forward_https.title: Forward HTTPS

forward_ssh.description: Let git use your host SSH keys for cloning and pushing
forward_ssh.title: Forward SSH

default_mode.description: bind mounts your project live (edits sync); snapshot copies it (isolated, disposable)
default_mode.title: Default Mode
